title: 3 AFT Reports
body_text:
3	AFT Reports
3.1	Account Activity and Balances Report 芒聙聯 AIBC/AIBU (BC), AIOC/AIOU (Ontario)
The Account Activity and Balances report is known as the AIBC/AIBU to BC financial institutions and as the AIOC/AIOU to Ontario financial institutions. The information in this report is also available on CAS Online. See the Central 1 Account Services (CAS) Online Part for information on accessing CAS Online.
Description
The Account Activity and Balances report provides a daily summary of all transactions posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the AIBC (for BC financial institutions) or the AIOC (for Ontario financial institutions). US dollar transactions are reported on the AIBU (for BC financial institutions) or the AIOU (for Ontario financial institutions). Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to all these reports. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Account Activity and Balances (AIBC/AIBU, AIOC/AIOU)
Purpose
For AFT purposes, financial institutions use this report to confirm postings of incoming and outgoing AFT transaction totals, including returns and AFT service charges.
Organization
If a financial institution has multiple Charters (163 and 162), the branches under Charter 163 (16310, 16320, 16330) may be reported on one page. The branches under Charter 162 (16210, 16220, 16230) may be reported on another.
The top section of the report lists the previous, opening, and closing balances, and the net activity. The middle section lists the postings by branch and by type. The lower section summarizes the total debit and credit postings to each branch. If the net AFT activity is balanced, meaning 0, there is no entry posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. If the net AFT activity is not balanced, either a debit or a credit entry will be posted.
Distribution
This report is produced on business days and is available to financial institutions the following day.
3.2	AFT Client芒聙聶s Daily Charges Report 芒聙聯 AFTD/UFTD
Description
The AFT Client芒聙聶s Daily Charges Report lists the total file charges and, if applicable, it also lists the one-time AFT setup and additional Originator ID fees associated with each Originator ID per day. These charges apply to Originators that upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT or the File and Report Exchange, or manually release files on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Note:	The one-time AFT setup and additional Originator ID fees are charged in Canadian funds, regardless of the currency of the AFT file.
Canadian dollar transaction fees are reported on the AFTD, and US dollar transaction fees are reported on the UFTD. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: AFT Client芒聙聶s Daily Charges Report (AFTD/UFTD)
Purpose
The financial institution uses this report to calculate the charge that applies to each Originator and, if applicable, manually posts it to the Originator芒聙聶s account. Financial institutions that post monthly should refer to the AFT Client芒聙聶s File Fee Monthly Summary Report (AFMS/UFMS).
Organization
This report is separated by branch and, when applicable, there can be two sections in the report: AFT file fee charges and AFT setup fee charges. The AFT setup fee charges section only appears if the financial institution has new AFT service setups that were requested and processed. In this section, there is one line per new AFT Originator. The Originator ID, Originator Name, and setup fee are listed and subtotalled.
In the AFT file fee charges section, there is one line per file creation number. The Originator ID, originator name, file creation number, and file charge are listed and subtotalled.
Distribution
This report is available to the financial institution on the business day after the file is submitted and/or after the AFT setup fee is charged. The report is only produced if an Originator from the branch submitted an AFT file and/or had a new AFT service setup.
3.3	AFT Client芒聙聶s File Fee Monthly Summary 芒聙聯 AFMS/UFMS (BC Only)
Description
The AFT Client芒聙聶s File Fee Monthly Summary lists the total file charges associated with each Originator ID per month. These charges apply to Originators that upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT or the File and Report Exchange, or manually release files on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the AFMS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UFMS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: AFT Client芒聙聶s File Fee Monthly Summary (AFMS/UFMS)
Purpose
The Originator is charged a fee for each outgoing AFT file submitted to Central 1. The financial institution uses this report to calculate the charge that applies to each Originator and, if applicable, manually posts it to the Originator芒聙聶s account. Financial institutions that post daily should refer to the AFT Client芒聙聶s Daily Charges Report (AFTD/UFTD).
Organization
This report is separated by branch and there is one line per Originator ID. The Originator ID, Originator name, number of transactions, and the total monthly charge for each Originator is provided.
Distribution
This report is produced monthly.
3.4	AFT Financial Institution/Client List 芒聙聯 L003/U003
Description
This report provides the financial institution with information on the Originator IDs that are assigned to the financial institution and its business members.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the L003, and US dollar transactions are reported on the U003. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: AFT Financial Institution/Client List 芒聙聯 L003/U003
Purpose
Use the report to provide AFT account managers with up-to-date details pertinent to their client芒聙聶s AFT configuration.
Organization
The report is sorted by Originator ID, company name, type of AFT service, file limits, offsetting entry information, and file fee costs to the financial institution.
Distribution
This report is produced on the last business day of each month.
3.5	AFT Monthly Fee Report 芒聙聯 AFTM/UFTM
Description
The AFT Monthly Fee Report lists the total monthly service charges and, if applicable, it also lists the one-time AFT setup and additional Originator ID fees associated with each Originator ID per month. These charges apply to Originators that upload files or automatically or manually release AFT files on
PaymentStream
AFT or have opted for the AFT Re-presentment service.
Note:	The one-time AFT setup and additional Originator ID fees are charged in Canadian funds, regardless of the currency of the AFT file.
Canadian dollar transaction fees are reported on the AFTM, and US dollar transaction fees are reported on the UFTM. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: AFT Monthly Fee (AFTM)
Purpose
The financial institution uses this report to calculate the charge that applies to each Originator and, if applicable, manually posts it to the Originator芒聙聶s account.
Organization
This report is separated by branch and, when applicable, there can be two sections in the report: AFT file fee charges and AFT setup fee charges. The setup fee charges section only appears if the financial institution has new AFT service setups that were requested and processed throughout the month. In this section, there is one line per new AFT Originator. The Originator ID, Originator Name, and setup fee are listed and subtotalled.
In the AFT file fee charges section, there is one line per Originator ID. The Originator ID, Originator name, number of files per month, number of transactions per file, and the total monthly charge for each Originator are provided.
Distribution
This report is produced monthly.
3.6	Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing 芒聙聯 CCD/UCD
Description
The Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing provides details on incoming AFT Transactions. This report also lists some exception transactions initiated by the financial institution or business member Originator. Paper transactions are not included on this report. The following types of AFT Transactions are reported:
AFT Transactions originated by another financial institution and destined to a financial institution member (C and D records)
The offsetting transaction for the outgoing AFT file, where applicable (C or D record)
AFT transactions returned by the receiving financial institution (I and J records)
Error corrections initiated by the sending financial institution and destined to the financial institution or business member originator (E and F records)
Recalled transactions initiated by the financial institution or business member Originator (I and J records with return code 903)
Offsetting transactions for AFT returns submitted by the financial institution via ORS or via the Batch Return Service, where the system does not create offsetting entries (I and J records)
Note:	This is an optional service that is only enabled upon request.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the CCD, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCD, per CR file in BC and per ON file in Ontario. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD)
Purpose
The transactions on each CCD/UCD report are included in the total amount of debits and credits at the bottom of the listing of transactions.
Use this report to perform the following:
View incoming AFT transactions to the branch
Review for incoming error corrections (E and F records) or incoming returns (I and J records)
Review offsetting credit transaction for an outgoing AFT re-presented debit (identified as 芒聙聹REPRESENT芒聙聺)
Verify that recalls initiated by the financial institution or business member originator were processed
Post manually if the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system is unable to facilitate this process
Determine when a transaction was posted to the member芒聙聶s account and to facilitate other tracing needs
Obtain the details necessary to return an AFT transaction
Organization
Each branch is listed separately. The report is structured as follows:
Transaction Details
芒聙聯
The top portion of the report is divided into six subsections by record type that list AFT Transactions. These are displayed only if transactions of that particular type exist. They are reported as follows:
芒聢聮
Credits (C)
芒聙聯 C records are incoming AFT credits
芒聢聮
Debits (D)
芒聙聯 D records are incoming AFT debits
芒聢聮
Debits (E)
芒聙聯 E records are error corrections (debits) initiated by another financial institution
芒聢聮
Credits (F)
芒聙聯 F records are error corrections (credits) initiated by another financial institution
芒聢聮
Credits (I)
芒聙聯 I records include the following:
芒聢聮
AFT credit transactions or credit error corrections returned by the destination financial institution, or
芒聢聮
Recalls of credit transactions initiated by the financial institution or business member originator, or
芒聢聮
the offsetting entry for an AFT return transaction submitted by the financial institution via ORS or via the Batch Return Service (where offsetting entries are not automatically created). This transaction detail is optional.
芒聢聮
Debits (J)
芒聙聯 J records include the following:
芒聢聮
AFT debit transactions or debit error corrections returned by the destination financial institution, or
芒聢聮
Recalls of debit transactions initiated by the financial institution or business member originator, or
芒聢聮
the offsetting entry for an AFT return transaction submitted by the financial institution via ORS or via the Batch Return Service (where offsetting entries are not automatically created). This transaction detail is optional.
Summary
芒聙聯
The file summary at the bottom of each report shows the net debit and credit amounts for each settlement period.
Financial institutions settle by file. The settlement is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. The amount of the settlement matches the file芒聙聶s amount. In general, financial institutions will have a single entry per business day.
Distribution
CCD
芒聙聯
This report is produced daily if incoming transactions (or applicable exception transactions) are received for the branch.
There are up to four versions of the CCD produced each day for BC and up to five versions of CCD for ON, one for each run. Each version is identified in the top left corner. For example, CR04 or ON04 for 09/15/18
is read as follows:
芒聢聮
CR04 or ON04 is the Run number
芒聢聮
09/15/18 is the date the transaction totals were posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account.
Each day芒聙聶s MERG file contains CCD reports in the order specified below.
BC Run
Ontario Run
Transaction Due Date
CR03
ON03
Previous business day or earlier
CR04
ON04
Previous business day or earlier
N/A
ONRT
Previous business day or earlier
NOTE: Ontario financial institutions will receive the ONRT report regardless of whether transactions are received.
CR01
ON01
Today or earlier
CR02
ON02
Today or earlier
Each day芒聙聶s CRF* report file also contains advance CCD reports in the order specified below.
Run
Transaction Due Date
CRF2
Current day or earlier
CRF3
Current day or earlier
CRF4
Current day or earlier
CRF1
Up to next day
UCD
芒聙聯
This report is produced Monday to Friday if incoming US dollar AFT Transactions, or applicable exception transactions, are received for the branch. There is one version of this report in BC per day (UC01). Financial institutions in Ontario receive the UN01 and the UNRT (returned US dollar AFT Transactions) reports.
3.7	Batch Returns Error List 芒聙聯 ICBE/UCBE
Description
The Batch Returns Error List provides a one-line summary of each transaction that is rejected from the financial institution芒聙聶s submitted AFT returns.
US dollar reports are only produced if the financial institution generates returns via the Batch Return Service and only if the software is capable of processing US dollar AFT returns.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICBE, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCBE. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Batch Returns Error List (ICBE/UCBE)
Purpose
The financial institution uses this report to identify transactions that are rejected from submitted AFT returns and to determine the reason for rejection. The ICBE/UCBE report shows transactions that are rejected during Central 1芒聙聶s initial edit. The Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections Report (ICRR/UCRR) shows transactions that rejected during Central 1芒聙聶s second edit.
Organization
The report is separated by branch. Each rejected transaction is reported on one line. The 23 characters in each batch input line are read as follows:
Character
Description
1
Transaction Type i.e.: c = credit, d = debit
2 芒聙聯 10
Return Reference Number
11 芒聙聯 20
Amount
21 芒聙聯 23
Return Reason Code
Distribution
The report is produced daily if a transaction within the file of AFT returns is rejected. It is available to the branch the following day.
3.8	Batch Returns User List 芒聙聯 ICBR/UCBR
Description
The Batch Returns User List displays accepted return transactions that have been submitted via ORS or the Batch Return Service. The report lists each transaction and the corresponding 9-digit reference number, amount, and return reason code.
If a financial institution produces AFT US dollar returns via the Batch Return Service and the financial institution芒聙聶s software is capable of processing AFT US dollar returns, the UCBR report is generated.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICBR, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCBR. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Batch Returns User List (ICBR/UCBR)
Purpose
The financial institution uses this report to identify transactions that were accepted in its submitted AFT returns.
Organization
The Batch Returns User List (ICBR/UCBR) report is generated for each ORS processing cycle with each cycle identified by a separate file creation number. The report is separated by Originator ID and file creation number. Each transaction is listed on one line. The total credit/debit item count and dollar value is provided at the bottom of the report. The transactions are sorted by the financial institution芒聙聶s Transit Number. If the file contains transactions from multiple branches, the transactions will be reported on separate pages.
Distribution
This report is produced daily if the financial institution submits AFT returns. It is available to the branch the following day.
3.9	Batch Returns User Summary List 芒聙聯 ICBS/UCBS
Description
The Batch Returns User Summary List provides a one-line summary of electronic AFT credit and debit returns for each branch, by Originator ID. The returns file is submitted via the Online Return System (ORS) or the Batch Return Service.
If a financial institution produces AFT US dollar returns via the Batch Return Service and the financial institution芒聙聶s software is capable of processing AFT US dollar returns, the UCBS report is generated.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICBS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCBS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Batch Returns User Summary List (ICBS/UCBS)
Purpose
The financial institution uses this report to verify, by branch, the total value and item count of accepted AFT returns submitted electronically.
Organization
The Batch Returns User Summary List (ICBS/UCBS) report is separated by Originator ID and file creation number. Multiple branches are listed per page if the file contains AFT returns for more than one branch. The total credit/debit item counts and dollar values are provided at the bottom of the report. This report is generated per ORS processing cycle and, as a result, there can be more than one report daily.
Distribution
This report is produced daily if the financial institution submits AFT returns. It is available to the financial institution the following day.
3.10	Business Pre-authorized Debits 芒聙聯 AFT 芒聙聯 PADB/UADB
Description
The Business Pre-authorized Debits 芒聙聯 AFT 芒聙聯 PADB/UADB report lists incoming electronic AFT Business PADs by branch.
Canadian dollar Business PAD transactions are reported on the PADB, and US dollar Business PAD transactions are reported on the UADB. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Business Pre-authorized Debits 芒聙聯 AFT (PADB/UADB)
Purpose
This report allows financial institutions to quickly view incoming Business PADs initiated from other financial institutions. Financial institutions that have a policy of notifying their members of Business PADs, can use this report to do so. See the CPA rules concerning the return times frames for Business PADs.
Organization
This report is separated by branch. Each Business PAD is reported on three lines and the details are the same as those on the CCD/UCD report.
Distribution
This report is produced if the branch receives a CCD/UCD report. The PADB/UADB report will be blank if no Business PADs were received. Each day芒聙聶s CRF* report file also contains advance PADB reports in the order specified in the table below.
Run
Transaction Due Date
CRF2
Current day or earlier
CRF3
Current day or earlier
CRF4
Current day or earlier
CRF1
Up to next day
3.11	Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections Report 芒聙聯 ICRR/UCRR
Description
The ICRR/UCRR report lists transactions that are:
Returned by the financial institution for AFT transactions that were originated by the business member originator or the financial institution
Recalled by the financial institution or business member originator before the transactions are sent to the receiving data centre. If a transaction is sent to the destination financial institution, but has not been posted, it will also appear on this report if it is successfully recalled.
Outgoing AFT transactions that are rejected by Central 1芒聙聶s second edit or by the receiving data centre are included in the ICRR/UCRR report
Error corrections that were used to return a transaction or a file that was posted to an account in error
Note: 	Error corrections do not guarantee the permanent return of funds because corrections can be disputed within 90 days of the posting date. If an item is disputed after this time, the matter is handled outside the Clearing.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICRR, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCRR. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections (ICRR/UCRR)
Purpose
Originators refer to the ICRR/UCRR report for the following reasons:
To identify outgoing AFT Transactions that are rejected and to determine the reason for rejection. If multiple transactions within a file are rejected, they will be reported on one page, and a grand total of rejected and accepted transactions will be displayed. Financial institutions use the report to manually post the rejected transactions back to the Originator芒聙聶s financial institution account since the transactions on this report are not provided to the banking service provider for automatic posting.
To identify transactions returned by the Receiving Financial Institution and to determine the reason for return
To confirm that Central 1 processed any Recalls requested by the Originator
To provide the list of error corrections which can be posted up to three business days after the transaction due date/date funds to be available on the date of exchange
Organization
Each Originator is listed separately. The report is structured as follows:
Transaction Details
芒聙聯 The top portion of the report lists the details of the AFT Transactions. The Tran column provides the reasons for return, recall, and error correction items, and the Invalid Field No. column gives the reason for a rejected item. Compare this number to the legend below the transaction details.
Field No.
Field Name
Explanation
04
Transaction type
The transaction type is invalid or blank. See CPA Standard 007 for a list of valid transaction codes.
05
Amount
The amount is blank.
06
Due Date/date funds to be available
The due date/date funds to be available is post-dated or Stale-Dated beyond the acceptable limits. Credit and/or debit transactions dated beyond 45 days of the file creation date are rejected. AFT Transactions backdated more than 173 calendar days (for debits) or 30 calendar days (for credits) are rejected.
07
Institutional identification number
Institution identification number is not in the correct format or does not exist.
08
Payor/Payee account number
Payor/Payee account number is invalid.
09*
Item trace number
Item trace number is invalid.
10*
Stored transaction type
Stored transaction type is invalid or blank.
11*
Originator short name
Originator short name is blank.
12*
Payor/Payee name
Payor/Payee name is blank.
13*
Originator long name
Originator long name is blank.
14*
Originating Direct Clearer User ID
Originating Direct Clearer User ID is invalid or blank.
15
Originating cross reference number
Cross reference number field is blank.
16
Institutional ID number for returns
Institutional ID number for returns is invalid or is not in the correct format. It should specify your financial institution.
17
Account number for returns
Account number for returns is invalid or is not in the correct format.
19*
Original item trace number
Original item trace number is invalid or blank.
21*
Invalid data element ID
Data element ID is invalid or blank.
51*
Unapproved insufficient funds
Central 1 internal use only.
60*
Original TXN not found for EFIJ
No match found.
61*
Duplicate E/F or I/J
Duplicate transaction.
62*
Institution in default
Financial institution is in default.
79*
Unapproved invalid account number
Central 1 internal use only.
99
I/J Reject, so reject corresponding
Original I or J return transaction rejected so that corresponding I or J Offsetting Transaction also rejected. Central 1 internal use only.
R*
Returned due to unacceptable destination
Central 1 internal use only.
*Contact Central 1 if you receive one of these error codes.
Summary
芒聙聯
The bottom portion of the report is divided into two columns: one for total number and value of debits (D, E, J) and one for total number and value of credits (C, F, I). The total Rejects, reversals, returns, and Recalls are listed followed by the grand total.
Note: 	Error corrections are included in the total for reversals.
Note: 	Return and recall totals are consolidated.
Note: 	Reject items will not be automatically posted on the ICRR report. As a result, a review of the rejected items and the Total Rejects field of the ICRR report is required to determine which rejected items must be manually posted. A warning message, 芒聙聹Please manually post as required,芒聙聺 follows the Total Rejects field, reminding financial institutions to review rejected items.
Distribution
This report is available the day after Central 1 has processed the file. Each day芒聙聶s ICR1 report file also contains advance ICRR reports.
Run
Transaction Due Date
ICR1
Current day
3.12	Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聯 AFT Clearing 芒聙聯 ICPS/UCPS
Description
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICPS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCPS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted.
This report provides a summary from individual CCD Reports corresponding to each Run. In BC, the Runs are indicated by CR01, CR02, CR03, and CR04. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: BC 芒聙聯 Daily AFT Clearing Settlement 芒聙聯 AFT Clearing (ICPS/UCPS)
In Ontario, the Runs are indicated by ON01, ON02, ON03, ON04, and ONRT. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Ontario 芒聙聯 Daily AFT Clearing Settlement 芒聙聯 AFT Clearing (ICPS/UCPS)
Financial institutions settle by file. The settlement is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. The amount of the settlement matches the file芒聙聶s amount. In general, financial institutions will have a single entry per file per business day.
This report gives the financial institution a breakdown of each transaction file delivered with a debit total (DR), a credit total (CR), and a net total per file. The report also provides an overall net settlement total. If the net total equals 0, there is no entry. If the net total does not equal 0, the report will post either a debit or credit. The net settlement reconciles to the 芒聙聹AS芒聙聺 AFT Settlement posting in the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICPS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCPS.
ICPS Report for Weekend AFT Clearing Files
Weekend AFT clearing files are listed on the following business day芒聙聶s ICPS report by Run number (BC 芒聙聯 CR0* files; Ontario 芒聙聯 ON0* and ONRT). The net totals for each of the Runs on the ICPS for the first business day following a weekend are a consolidation of the AFT Runs from the weekend up to the first business day of the week. To reconcile the ICPS report to the weekend CCD reports, you must add the CCD reports by CR0* or ON0* Run numbers for Saturday, Sunday, and Monday.
The net total from each weekend AFT Run reconciles to the 芒聙聹AS芒聙聺 AFT Settlement posting in the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account.
Purpose
Financial institutions use the report to obtain a summary of the individual CCD/UCD reports corresponding to each AFT Clearing file (BC 芒聙聯 CR0* files; Ontario 芒聙聯 ON0* and ONRT). Financial institutions also use this report to assist with the reconciliation of posting to their Central account. The ICPS also reports settlement for the incoming files for those transactions due.
Organization
The report is produced by the financial institution head office and sorted by branch and by clearing Run. If a financial institution settles by multiple Charters (e.g., 163 and 162), the branches under Charter 163 (16310, 16320, 16330) will be reported on one page. The branches under Charter 162 (16210, 16220, 16230) will be reported on another.
Distribution
The report is produced daily, on business days.
3.13	Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聯 AFT Originating 芒聙聯 ICPS/UCPS
Description
The Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聯 AFT Originating provides a breakdown of AFT transactions originated by Originator ID. It also gives a debit (DR) total, a credit (CR) total, and a net settlement amount. If the Originator is registered in the offsetting entry feature, there is no net settlement amount. If there is an adjustment (e.g., a transaction fails the edit check, etc.), the net amount will show in the net settlement column and as an entry in the financial institution Central 1 current account. The net settlement reconciles to the 芒聙聹AO芒聙聺 AFT Outgoing Settlement posting in the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICPS, and US dollar Transactions are reported on the UCPS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Daily AFT Clearing Settlement 芒聙聯 AFT Originating (ICPS/UCPS)
Purpose
Use this report to map posting activities and reconcile current accounts. This report displays the settlement to current accounts and provides financial institutions with a summary of transactions created by Originator ID芒聙聶s.
Organization
The report is sorted by Transit Number and Originator IDs attached to that Transit Number, credit total (items and amount), debit total (items and amount), and net total (items and amount) of files.
Distribution
Available the business day after Central 1 has settled outgoing transactions.
3.14	Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聯 AFT Returns 芒聙聯 ICPS/UCPS
Description
The Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聯 AFT Returns provides settlement information for AFT returns initiated by the financial institution. If a financial institution has the offsetting entry feature enabled, there is no net settlement entry shown on the report or posted to the current account. For financial institutions that do not have the offsetting entry and do not use Central 1芒聙聶s return offset feature, the non-zero net amount value will show on the Settled to Account column and will be the posted amount. The net settlement reconciles to the 芒聙聹AR芒聙聺 AFT Returns posting in the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICPS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCPS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Daily AFT Clearing Settlement 芒聙聯 AFT Returns (ICPS/UCPS)
Purpose
Use this report to obtain a summary of returns associated with the financial institution. It can be used by financial institutions to reconcile current accounts.
Organization
The report is sorted by Transit Number and Return Originator ID芒聙聶s, credit total (items and amount), debit total (items and amount), and net total (items and amount) per returns submitted by financial institutions.
Distribution
Available the day after Central 1 processed the returns submitted.
3.15	Item Trace Report 芒聙聯 Incoming Items 芒聙聯 ICTI/UCTI
Description
The Item Trace Report 芒聙聯 Incoming Items provides a detailed list of an originator芒聙聶s outgoing AFT transactions by file creation number. If applicable, the report also shows any offsetting transactions that Central 1 created. Additionally, it lists transactions generated for Originator IDs that have opted for the AFT Re-presentment service.
This report also provides a detailed list of AFT transactions returned by the financial institution via ORS or the Batch Return Service. To request this report for AFT returns, contact Central 1.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICTI, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCTI. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Incoming AFT Transactions List (ICTI/UCTI)
Purpose
Use the report to view the individual transactions sent out to the Receiving Financial Institutions and to view any offsetting transactions created by Central 1.
Use the report to view AFT transactions returned by the financial institution via ORS or the Batch Return Service, if the financial institution requests the report for returned AFT transactions.
Organization
The report is separated by Originator ID and File Creation Number and is sorted by cross reference number. Each transaction is listed separately.
If applicable, a separate report is produced for:
AFT transactions returned by the financial institution
Originators that have opted for the AFT Re-presentment service
Distribution
Available the business day after Central 1 processes the file.
3.16	Monthly AFT Transaction Volumes Report 芒聙聯 ICTV/UCTV
Description
This report provides financial institutions with monthly volumes for incoming AFT transactions (AFT clearing), AFT returns, and outgoing AFT transactions (AFT Originating).
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICTV, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCTV. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Monthly AFT Transaction Volumes Report (ICTV/UCTV)
Purpose
Use the report to validate the total monthly charges for AFT transaction volumes.
Organization
The report is sorted by financial institution and the transits associated with that financial institution. The Credit Clearing, AFT Return, and AFT Originating columns are further divided by the number of credits and debits. The Total field provides the total of credits and debits. If a financial institution settles by multiple Charters (e.g., 163 and 162), the branches under Charter 163 (16310, 16320, 16330) will be reported on one page. The branches under Charter 162 (16210, 16220, 16230) will be reported on another page.
Distribution
This report is produced monthly.
3.17	Monthly Customer AFT Billing Report 芒聙聯 ICCB/UCCB
Description
The Monthly Customer AFT Billing Report provides the monthly charges for each Originator. The report also provides details of transaction volumes, returns/Rejects, and
PaymentStream
AFT user fees (file uploads and monthly user fees).
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICCB, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCCB. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Monthly Customer AFT Billing Report (ICCB/UCCB)
Purpose
Use the report to view and validate total monthly charges for AFT Originators.
Organization
Reports are produced by Originator ID. Charges for outgoing AFT transactions and Returns and Rejects are included on the report. Transaction volumes are also provided. The content is sorted by AFT record type. This report also lists the monthly charges for manual release/file upload, automatic release, and the AFT Re-presentment service.
Distribution
This report is produced on the last business day of the month.
3.18	NOC 芒聙聯 Originated by Credit Union 芒聙聯 NCOC/NUOC
Description
The NOC 芒聙聯 Originated by financial institution report lists AFT notice of change (NOC) transactions created by the financial institution. It provides the old and new Transit Number and account number as applicable. This information is sent to the Originator via its financial institution. The Originator uses the NOC to update its database so that future AFT transactions are sent with the correct information.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the NCOC, and US dollar transactions are reported on the NUOC. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: NOC 芒聙聯 Originated by Financial Institution
Purpose
Use this report to view NOC transactions created by the financial institution.
Organization
The report is produced by Transit Number. The details of each NOC transaction are listed on two lines.
Distribution
The report is produced only if the financial institution creates NOC transactions. It is available on the following business day.
3.19	Notice of Change (AFT) Detailed Listing 芒聙聯 NCDL/NUDL
Description
The Notice of Change (AFT) Detailed Listing provides updated AFT transaction information (e.g., recipient account number and name) from the destination financial institution to the financial institution or business member Originator.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the NCDL, and US dollar transactions are reported on the NUDL. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Notice of Change (AFT) Detailed Listing (NCDL/NUDL)
Purpose
Financial institutions and business member Originators use this information to update their database of outgoing AFT Transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT or on alternative software.
Organization
The report is separated by Originator ID. Each transaction is reported on two lines.
Distribution
The report is available to the financial institution or business member Originator if NOC transactions were received for that Originator ID. The report is produced weekly.
3.20	Settlement Register 芒聙聯 ICSR/UCSR
Description
The Settlement Register lists the amounts that will be debited or credited to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account by branch. This applies to original outgoing AFT files, returned files, and re-presentment files. This report also lists transactions rejected from original outgoing AFT files and specifies whether Central 1 or the Receiving Financial Institution rejected them. Returned item transactions that reject during Central 1芒聙聶s second edit are also reported here.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICSR, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCSR. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Settlement Register (ICSR/UCSR)
Purpose
Financial institutions use the ICSR/UCSR report to verify AFT postings to their Central 1 accounts.
Organization
The report is separated by Originator ID and branch. There are two sections in the report 芒聙聯 Our Deliveries and Our Receipts. Each section has file creation numbers. If the file contains transactions for more than one day, the transaction date is provided. The file creation numbers are broken down by debits (DR) and credits (CR). The total of debits and credits will be netted. If the net total equals 0, there is no entry. If the net total does not equal 0, the report will post either a debit or credit.
The Our Receipts section displays the transactions settled on a particular due date when both credits and debits are sent by the originator. The Our Deliveries section displays any transaction that has been returned or rejected. A credit is any credit transaction that is either rejected by or returned to the originator. A debit is a transaction that is returned to the financial institution.
Our Deliveries Due to/Due from is netted into one subtotal. Our Receipts Due to/Due from is netted into one subtotal. The total debits and credits are netted and if the net is balanced, meaning 0, no entry is posted. The code for posting settlement for AFT Originating files is AO. The code for posting settlement for originating AFT returns is AR.
Distribution
This report is available the day after the file Settlement Date. Returns are also reported on the following business day. Returns sent on Saturday are listed on Monday芒聙聶s report, which is available on Tuesday.
3.21	Summary of Automated Transfer System 芒聙聯 CCS/UCS
Description
The Summary of Automated Transfer System lists the settlement totals for incoming AFT Transactions by Settlement Date and by Run. Multiple branches are reported on each page.
In BC, financial institutions settle by file posting date. The settlement is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. The amount of the settlement matches the file芒聙聶s net settlement amount.
In Ontario, financial institutions settle by due date. Transactions due on the previous or current day are posted on the current day and future-dated transactions will be posted on the due date.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the CCS, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCS. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: BC 芒聙聯 Summary of Automated Transfer System (CCS/UCS)
Purpose
Multi-branch financial institutions use this report to obtain a summary of incoming AFT item counts and dollar values for each branch.
Organization
The upper section provides the credit and debit item count and dollar value of incoming AFT Transactions. These totals are obtained from the CCD/UCD report that lists the transaction details.
The lower section provides the total credit and debit amount for the financial institution芒聙聶s branches by Settlement Date and Run. If a financial institution has multiple Charters (e.g.,163 and 162), the branches under Charter 163 (16310, 16320, 16330) will be reported on one page. The branches under Charter 162 (16210, 16220, 16230) will be reported on another.
Note:	The amounts are posted by branch, not by the total on the bottom of the page.
Distribution
The CCS is produced daily but only if the financial institution received AFT Transactions the previous day. Each day芒聙聶s CRF* report file also contains advance CCS reports in the order specified below:
Run
Transaction Due Date
CRF2
Current day or earlier
CRF3
Current day or earlier
CRF4
Current day or earlier
CRF1
Up to next day
The UCS is produced Monday to Friday but only if the financial institution received AFT Transactions the previous business day.
3.22	Summary of Data Delivered from Auto Re-presentment 芒聙聯 ICDN/UCDN
Description
The Summary of Data Delivered report lists AFT re-presented debits and the offsetting credit transactions that are created for the AFT Re-presentment Originator.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on ICDN, and US dollar transactions are reported on UCDN. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Summary of Data Delivered from Auto Re-presentment ICDN/UCDN)
Purpose
This report is used to advise and confirm that Central 1 generated and processed AFT re-presented transactions for the AFT Originator that requested the AFT Re-presentment service.
Organization
This report is produced by the AFT Re-presentment Originator and by File Creation Number. It is divided into the following three sections:
Payment Details
芒聙聯 Displays total number and dollar value of transactions in the file.
Error Correction Details
芒聙聯 This section always shows zeros.
Totals
芒聙聯 This section lists the total credit/debit item counts and dollar values in the AFT Re-presentment file.
Distribution
This report is produced only if a financial institution or business member Originator has requested the AFT Re-presentment Service and a pre-authorized debit has been returned for AFT reason codes 901 NSF and/or 908 Funds Not Cleared and is eligible for Re-presentment.
3.23	Summary of Data Delivered from Batch Returns 芒聙聯 ICDR/UCDR
Description
The Summary of Data Delivered from the Batch Returns report provides a summarized list of AFT returned transactions submitted to Central聽1 by AFT return originators via ORS or via the Batch Return Service.
Canadian dollar AFT returns are reported on ICDR and US dollar AFT returns are reported on UCDR. Since the ICDR and UCDR reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information provided applies to both reports (except where otherwise noted). See Figure 24.
Figure 24: Batch Returns Summary Data (ICDR)
Purpose
This report is used to advise and confirm that Central聽1 processed AFT return transactions from AFT Return Originators.
Organization
This report is produced by the AFT Return Originator ID and by File Creation Number. It is divided into three sections:
Payment Details
芒聙聯 Displays total number and dollar value of transactions in the file.
Error Correction Details 芒聙聯 This section always shows zeros.
Totals 芒聙聯 This section lists the total credit/debit item counts and dollar values in the AFT Return file.
Distribution
This report is produced daily if AFT returns were submitted the previous day.
3.24	Summary of Data Received from AFT System 芒聙聯 ICRM/UCRM
Description
The Summary of Data Received from AFT System report lists outgoing AFT Transactions by Originator ID and file creation number. Additionally, it lists transactions generated for Originator IDs that have opted for the AFT Re-presentment service. It also lists transactions returned via the Online Return System (ORS) or via the Batch Return Service by File Creation Number. The title of the report varies according to which system is used to create the file of AFT Transactions. For example, the title may read 芒聙聹Summary of Data Received from AFT System芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Summary of Data Received from Batch Returns芒聙聺.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICRM, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCRM. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Summary of Data Received from AFT System (ICRM/UCRM)
Purpose
The Originator uses this report to obtain a detailed listing of outgoing AFT Transactions by File Creation Number.
The financial institution uses this report to obtain a detailed listing of returned transactions, submitted via ORS or the Batch Return Service, by File Creation Number.
Organization
This report is produced by the Originator, by File Creation Number. It is structured as follows:
Payment Details
芒聙聯
Displays total number and dollar value of transactions in the file. Separated by Direct Clearer.
Error Correction Details
芒聙聯
Always shows zeros.
Totals
芒聙聯
Displays the total dollar value and item count of AFT Transactions.
Distribution
This report is produced daily if outgoing AFT files or AFT returns were submitted the previous day.
3.25	Summary of Reversed Error Corrections Report 芒聙聯 ICSE/UCSE
Description
The Summary of Reversed Error Corrections Report provides the total credit/debit item count and dollar value of error corrections requested by a financial institution or business member originator.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICSE, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCSE. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 26.
Figure 26: Summary of Reversed Error Corrections (ICSE/UCSE)
Purpose
This report is used to confirm that Central 1 processed the originator芒聙聶s error correction request.
Organization
This report is produced by the Originator, by File Creation Number. It is divided into the following three sections:
Payment Details
芒聙聯 This section will always show zeros.
Error Correction Details
芒聙聯 This section lists the credit/debit item counts and dollar values by financial institution.
Totals
芒聙聯 This section lists the total credit/debit item counts and dollar values in the Error Correction file.
Distribution
This report is produced only if a financial institution or business member originator requests an error correction the previous day.
3.26	Transaction Account Warning List 芒聙聯 ICRW/UCRW
Description
The Transaction Account Warning List contains outgoing AFT transactions that may contain an error in the destination account number. If the account number validation is the only error, the transaction will be sent to the Receiving Financial Institution, but a warning message will also be sent to the AFT Originator. However, there is no guarantee that the Receiving Financial Institution will process the AFT transaction.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the ICRW, and US dollar transactions are reported on the UCRW. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports except where otherwise noted. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: Transaction Account Warning List (ICRW/UCRW)
Purpose
Use the report to investigate and correct account numbers for accurate processing.
Organization
Reports are produced by Originator ID, by File Creation Number. The details of each transaction include the financial institution芒聙聶s Transit Number, the name of the Payee/drawee, date of the transaction, the amount, the item trace number, and the account number. This report also provides the debit total (number and amount) and credit total (number and amount) of the original file.
Distribution
The report is available the business day after Central 1 processes the file and is only produced if there are potential account number errors.
END
